_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use spelling rules, word parts, patterns and word origins to spell accurately and independently — including when using a dictionary or digital tools.

_source.w: We are learning to use spelling rules, word parts, patterns and word origins to spell accurately and independently — including when using a dictionary or digital tools.
y: Independent, accurate spelling demonstrates control over written language.
Using multiple spelling strategies gives us the best tools for any word we encounter.
Being able to use reference tools efficiently supports lifelong learning.
eg: We apply spelling rules and generalisations to spell unfamiliar words.
We use knowledge of word origins — Latin, Greek, French — to inform our spelling of complex words.
We use dictionaries and digital tools to verify and correct our spelling.
We identify patterns in words from the same origin and use them to spell related words.
We spell subject-specific vocabulary accurately across all our school work.
We develop personal strategies for the words we consistently find challenging.
